work_unit: ota_workflow: OTA-M-002 monitor 恢复告警一次性 (recovered event 永不清理) + OTA-L-002 DiscordNotifier 部分发送重发 (后续块失败 mark event 走冷却而非每轮重发已发块) (stacked, same file monitor.py)
situation: audit-2026-07-05-008 derived fix #5 (priority 5, severity medium, kind=route-fix) + #10 (priority 10, severity low, kind=route-fix), same file monitor.py → stacked in one commit. OTA-M-002: _record_service_health (monitor.py:510-513) on down→up transition calls _send_alert(event_key=`service:{name}:recovered`, repeat=False); _should_send_event(repeat=False) returns True only when no event entry exists; after first recovery _mark_event_sent writes the recovered event into state.events, and it is NEVER cleared thereafter → second down→up recovery: _should_send_event finds the stale recovered event → returns False → recovery alert silently dropped forever. OTA-L-002: DiscordNotifier.send (monitor.py:131-139) loops chunks calling _post_message which raises on raise_for_status; if chunk N>1 fails the exception propagates out of send → _send_alert catches in `except Exception`, sent stays False → _mark_event_sent NOT called → event stays unsent → next monitor round _should_send_event returns True again → resend ALL chunks including already-sent chunk 1 → users get duplicate chunk-1 spam every round until the failing chunk succeeds.
action: 1) OTA-M-002: in _record_service_health, added a `down`-transition cleanup block right before constructing the `service:{name}:down` event_key — pop `service:{service_name}:recovered` from state.events (and write back the dict) so the next down→up recovery re-triggers _should_send_event(repeat=False)=True. Comment explains the repeat=False one-shot semantics. 2) OTA-L-002: rewrote DiscordNotifier.send to catch per-chunk exceptions with a sent_count counter: if the FIRST chunk (the one carrying attachments) fails → re-raise (full failure, _send_alert does NOT mark event, next round retries the whole alert cleanly); if a LATER chunk fails after sent_count>=1 → log a partial-failure warning via module logger and return True (partial success) so _send_alert marks the event → next round for repeat=True events respects repeat_alert_minutes cooldown instead of rebroadcasting already-sent chunks every round. Single-file change, route-fix → reused opt-ota_workflow-1783171357 worktree (stacked on 124853a = OTA-M-001+OTA-L-003).
outcome_evidence: opt-worktree.sh reported '→ 复用同 area worktree (route-fix): opt-ota_workflow-1783171357' and '✓ 提交到 opt-ota_workflow-1783171357 方向=ota_workflow:monitor-recovery'. git -C opt-ota_workflow-1783171357 log --oneline shows new HEAD 1ed036b stacked directly on 124853a (OTA-M-001+OTA-L-003) on 9bfff05 (OTA-H-001c+M-004) on 9424759 (OTA-H-001b) on 0ca59a6 (OTA-H-001a). python3 ast.parse on monitor.py → 'syntax OK'. Main checkout restored to pre-edit baseline by opt-worktree cp/restore (expected; fix lives on branch auto/opt-ota_workflow-1783171357 HEAD=1ed036b, pushed to origin automatically by cmd_commit). Fix verified on worktree: _record_service_health down-branch now pops `service:{name}:recovered` before building down event_key; DiscordNotifier.send uses sent_count counter, re-raises on first-chunk failure, returns True on later-chunk failure after >=1 sent, returns sent_count>0 on full success.
